name: Tulsi
preparation: Boil 10-12 fresh tulsi leaves in 1 cup water for 5 minutes. Add honey and ginger if desired.
safety_notes: Avoid large doses during pregnancy; may interact with blood-thinning medication.

name: Ashwagandha
preparation: Mix 1 teaspoon of ashwagandha powder in warm milk or water before bed.
safety_notes: Avoid in pregnancy; consult a doctor if on thyroid or sedative medication.

name: Turmeric
preparation: Mix 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder in warm milk or water.
safety_notes: May increase bleeding risk with blood thinners; avoid high doses before surgery.

name: Neem
key_benefits: Skin health, blood purification, dental care
preparation: Boil a handful of neem leaves in water and use as a wash, or take neem leaf extract as directed.
safety_notes: Avoid during pregnancy and in children under 12 without guidance; not for prolonged internal use.

name: Aloe Vera
preparation: Extract fresh gel from an aloe leaf and apply topically, or take 1-2 tbsp of pure aloe juice.
dosage: 1-2 tbsp juice daily, or apply gel as needed
safety_notes: Internal use in excess can cause digestive upset; avoid during pregnancy.

name: Brahmi
preparation: Mix 1/2 teaspoon brahmi powder in warm water or milk.
safety_notes: May cause mild digestive upset initially; consult a doctor if on thyroid medication.

name: Ginger
preparation: Boil a few slices of fresh ginger in water for 5-10 minutes.
safety_notes: Avoid high doses with blood-thinning medication or gallstones.

name: Mint
preparation: Steep fresh mint leaves in hot water for 5 minutes.
safety_notes: Generally safe; avoid excess in acid reflux-prone individuals.

name: Hibiscus
preparation: Boil dried hibiscus petals in water for 10 minutes to make tea, or grind fresh flowers into a hair paste.
dosage: 1 cup tea daily, or use paste weekly
safety_notes: May lower blood pressure; use caution if already on blood pressure medication.

name: Rose
preparation: Steep dried rose petals in hot water for 5-7 minutes to make tea.
safety_notes: Generally safe; check for pollen allergies before topical use.

name: Papaya
preparation: Eat 1 cup of ripe papaya, or use raw papaya in cooking.
safety_notes: Avoid raw/unripe papaya during pregnancy.

name: Curry Leaf
preparation: Chew 8-10 fresh curry leaves in the morning, or add to cooking.
safety_notes: Generally safe in food amounts; no major concerns.

name: Peepal
preparation: Boil peepal bark or leaves in water for 10 minutes and strain.
safety_notes: Limited modern research; use under guidance for internal use.

name: Bamboo
preparation: Bamboo shoot extract or powder as per product instructions.
safety_notes: Ensure shoots are properly processed; raw bamboo shoots can be toxic.

name: Marigold
preparation: Crush fresh marigold petals into a paste and apply to minor wounds or skin irritation.
safety_notes: For external use only; discontinue if irritation occurs.

name: Onion
preparation: Consume raw or cooked onion regularly, or extract juice for topical use.
safety_notes: Raw onion juice may irritate sensitive skin; patch test before topical use.

name: Amla
preparation: Eat 1-2 fresh amla fruits, or take 1 tsp amla powder with water.
safety_notes: Generally very safe; reduce dose if it causes acidity in sensitive individuals.

name: Fenugreek
key_benefits: Blood sugar support, digestion, lactation support
preparation: Soak 1 tsp fenugreek seeds overnight in water, drink the water and seeds in the morning.
safety_notes: May lower blood sugar; caution if on diabetes medication.

name: Cinnamon
preparation: Add 1/4 tsp cinnamon powder to warm water or tea.
safety_notes: Avoid high doses in liver conditions; use cassia cinnamon cautiously.

name: Black Pepper
key_benefits: Digestion, respiratory support, enhances nutrient absorption
preparation: Add a pinch of fresh ground black pepper to warm water or food.
safety_notes: Avoid excess in acid reflux or ulcer conditions.

name: Fennel
preparation: Chew 1 tsp fennel seeds after meals, or steep in hot water for tea.
safety_notes: Generally very safe; mild allergy possible in rare cases.

name: Coriander
preparation: Soak 1 tsp coriander seeds in water overnight, strain and drink in the morning.
safety_notes: Generally safe in normal food and remedy amounts.

name: Cardamom
preparation: Add 1-2 crushed cardamom pods to tea or warm water.
safety_notes: Generally safe; avoid excess if gallstones are present.

name: Licorice
preparation: Boil a small piece of licorice root in water for 10 minutes, strain and drink.
safety_notes: Avoid prolonged use or high doses in high blood pressure; not for long-term daily use.

name: Holy Basil Krishna Tulsi
preparation: Boil 8-10 fresh leaves in 1 cup water for 5 minutes.
safety_notes: Same cautions as regular Tulsi; avoid large doses during pregnancy.

name: Shatavari
preparation: Mix 1/2 tsp shatavari powder in warm milk.
safety_notes: Consult a doctor if pregnant or on hormone-related medication.

name: Guduchi
preparation: Boil a small piece of giloy stem in water for 10-15 minutes, strain and drink.
safety_notes: Avoid in autoimmune conditions without medical guidance; may lower blood sugar.

name: Vetiver
preparation: Soak vetiver roots in water and use the infused water for drinking or bathing.
safety_notes: Generally safe; primarily used for cooling in hot weather.

name: Jasmine
key_benefits: Stress relief, skin care, calming aroma
preparation: Steep dried jasmine flowers in hot water for 5 minutes to make tea.
safety_notes: Generally safe; check for flower allergies before topical use.

name: Lemongrass
preparation: Boil fresh or dried lemongrass stalks in water for 5-10 minutes.
safety_notes: Generally safe; avoid excessive daily long-term use.

name: Basil Sweet Basil
key_benefits: Digestion, respiratory support, cooling with seeds
preparation: Soak 1 tsp basil seeds (sabja) in water until they swell, add to drinks.
safety_notes: Ensure seeds are fully soaked before consuming to avoid choking risk.

name: Triphala
scientific_name: Emblica officinalis, Terminalia bellirica, Terminalia chebula (blend)
preparation: Mix 1/2 to 1 tsp triphala powder in warm water before bed.
safety_notes: May cause mild loose stools initially; reduce dose if needed. Avoid in pregnancy without guidance.

name: Manjistha
key_benefits: Skin health, blood purification, lymphatic support
preparation: Boil 1/2 tsp manjistha root powder in water for 10 minutes, strain and drink.
safety_notes: Avoid during pregnancy; consult a doctor if on blood-thinning medication.

name: Amalaki Guduchi Blend
preparation: Mix 1 tsp combined powder in warm water.
safety_notes: Generally safe; consult a doctor if on immunosuppressant medication.

name: Vidanga
preparation: Take 1/4 tsp vidanga powder with warm water after meals.
safety_notes: Use only in small amounts; avoid prolonged use without guidance.

name: Punarnava
key_benefits: Kidney support, mild diuretic, swelling relief
preparation: Boil 1 tsp punarnava powder or root in water for 10 minutes.
safety_notes: Avoid in low blood pressure conditions; consult a doctor for kidney concerns.

name: Bhringraj
preparation: Mix bhringraj oil into the scalp, or take 1/2 tsp powder with water.
dosage: Apply oil 2-3 times weekly, or 1/2 tsp powder daily
safety_notes: Generally safe for topical use; patch test before full application.

name: Kalonji Black Seed
preparation: Take 1/4 tsp kalonji seeds or oil with warm water.
safety_notes: Avoid high doses during pregnancy; consult a doctor if on diabetes medication.

name: Moringa
preparation: Add 1 tsp moringa leaf powder to water, smoothies, or food.
safety_notes: Generally safe in food amounts; avoid root/bark extracts during pregnancy.

name: Indian Gooseberry Leaves
preparation: Boil a handful of amla leaves in water for 10 minutes and strain.
safety_notes: Generally safe in moderate amounts.

name: Sandalwood
preparation: Make a paste of sandalwood powder with water or rose water and apply to skin.
safety_notes: For external use only unless using food-grade preparations under guidance.

name: Henna
key_benefits: Hair conditioning, scalp cooling, mild dye
preparation: Mix henna powder with water into a paste and apply to hair or skin.
safety_notes: Patch test before use; avoid contact with eyes.

name: Dill
key_benefits: Digestion, gas relief, mild sedative for infants
preparation: Steep fresh or dried dill leaves/seeds in hot water for 5-10 minutes.
safety_notes: Generally safe; use diluted preparations for infants under guidance.

name: Bael
preparation: Boil bael fruit pulp in water or make a sherbet with ripe fruit.
safety_notes: Avoid excess unripe fruit, which can be too astringent.

name: Guggul
preparation: Take guggul resin extract as directed, typically in tablet or powder form.
safety_notes: Avoid during pregnancy; consult a doctor if on thyroid or cholesterol medication.

name: Kutki
preparation: Take 1/4 tsp kutki powder with warm water.
safety_notes: Use under guidance for liver conditions; avoid prolonged self-use.

name: Jatamansi
preparation: Mix 1/4 tsp jatamansi powder in warm milk before bed.
safety_notes: Generally safe; may cause mild drowsiness, avoid before driving.

name: Vacha
preparation: Take a small pinch of vacha powder with honey.
safety_notes: Use only in very small amounts; not for prolonged daily use without guidance.

name: Arjuna
preparation: Boil 1 tsp arjuna bark powder in water or milk for 10 minutes.
safety_notes: Consult a doctor if on heart medication before use.

name: Haritaki
preparation: Take 1/2 tsp haritaki powder with warm water before bed.
safety_notes: May cause mild loose stools initially; avoid in pregnancy without guidance.

name: Bibhitaki
preparation: Take 1/2 tsp bibhitaki powder with warm water.
safety_notes: Generally safe in recommended doses.

name: Kokum
preparation: Make a kokum sherbet by soaking dried kokum rind in water with a little jaggery.
safety_notes: Generally safe as a food-based remedy.

name: Nutmeg
key_benefits: Sleep support, digestion, mild pain relief
preparation: Add a small pinch of nutmeg powder to warm milk before bed.
safety_notes: Avoid large doses; excess nutmeg can be toxic.

name: Clove
key_benefits: Dental pain relief, digestion, respiratory support
preparation: Chew a whole clove or apply diluted clove oil to the gums for toothache.
dosage: 1 clove as needed, or diluted oil topically
safety_notes: Undiluted clove oil can irritate gums; dilute before topical use.

name: Ajwain
preparation: Boil 1 tsp ajwain seeds in water for 5 minutes and strain.
safety_notes: Avoid excess during pregnancy.

name: Chirayata
preparation: Soak 1 tsp chirayata in water overnight, strain and drink in the morning.
safety_notes: Very bitter; use under guidance for fever or liver concerns.

name: Pomegranate
preparation: Eat fresh pomegranate seeds or drink fresh juice.
safety_notes: Generally very safe as a food-based remedy.

name: Drumstick Leaves
preparation: Cook fresh moringa leaves into food, or steep dried leaves for tea.
dosage: 1 cup cooked leaves or tea daily
safety_notes: Generally safe in food amounts.

name: Wheatgrass
preparation: Juice fresh wheatgrass shoots, drink on an empty stomach.
safety_notes: May cause nausea in some; start with a small amount.

name: Betel Leaf
preparation: Chew a fresh betel leaf after meals.
safety_notes: Avoid with tobacco or areca nut, which are harmful; leaf alone in moderation is traditional.

name: Indian Sarsaparilla
preparation: Boil 1 tsp root powder in water for 10 minutes and strain.
safety_notes: Generally safe in recommended doses.
